content:
{
  "title": "Contextual Consistency Prompting for Robust LLM Defense Against Jailbreaks",
  "problem": "Large language models are vulnerable to adversarial prompts and jailbreak attempts that circumvent safety guardrails. Existing prompting strategies often fail to maintain robust defense without degrading utility.",
  "motivation": "Robustness against adversarial misuse is critical as LLMs are increasingly deployed in high-stakes settings. Attackers exploit inconsistencies in model behavior across paraphrased or contextually shifted prompts, highlighting a need for adaptive, context-aware defenses. Addressing this gap can significantly enhance LLM safety without excessive retraining.",
  "method": "We propose Contextual Consistency Prompting (CCP), a meta-prompting strategy that augments user input with paraphrased and contextually perturbed variants of the original prompt within the same input batch. The LLM is instructed to produce responses that are both safe and consistent across these variants, explicitly flagging or abstaining when contradictions or policy violations are detected. This approach leverages the model's own capabilities for consistency checking and self-reflection, discouraging jailbreaks that rely on subtle manipulations.",
  "experiment_plan": "Evaluate CCP on adversarially constructed jailbreak datasets (e.g., JailbreakBench, AdvBench) and standard harmless prompt datasets. Compare against baseline defense prompting (e.g., reinforcement learning with human feedback, safety classifiers) using metrics such as jailbreak success rate, utility preservation, and response consistency. Analyze effectiveness across a spectrum of paraphrased and obfuscated attack inputs."
}
